blockingIssues.description: Dependencies section in ARCHITECTURE.md (line ~565) lists 'ai' package (^6.0.55) but it is not in package.json dependencies. The standalone-server.ts uses native Node.js streams, not the 'ai' SDK.

Health endpoint response examples in all three client docs (OPENCODE.md, OH-MY-PI.md, FACTORY-DROID.md) show `{"status":"ok","version":"2.3.20","auth":"not_authenticated"}` but actual implementation returns additional `mcp: {enabled, servers, tools}` field. Users will see unexpected fields causing confusion.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on runtime documentation conventions: should documentation always specify both bun AND npm/node alternatives, or pick one primary runtime and note the other as fallback? The readme-documentation reviewer noted this ambiguity.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: readme-documentation reviewer found README assumes bun exclusively with no mention of npm alternatives despite package.json supporting both.
